name: Amphan
description: One of the strongest cyclones to hit West Bengal in decades, causing massive destruction in the Sundarbans and Kolkata.

name: Fani
description: A rare pre-monsoon extremely severe cyclonic storm that made landfall near Puri with winds gusting up to 200 km/h.

name: Phailin
description: Prompted India's largest evacuation in 23 years, significantly minimizing casualties despite its massive intensity.

name: Hudhud
description: Directly hit the major city of Visakhapatnam, causing extensive damage to urban infrastructure and trees.

name: Vardah
description: A severe storm that crossed the Chennai coast, uprooting thousands of trees and disrupting the city's power grid for days.

name: Ockhi
description: Notable for its unusual path and rapid intensification, causing heavy losses to the fishing community in the Arabian Sea.

name: Gaja
description: Devastated the agricultural delta region of Tamil Nadu, particularly affecting coconut groves and orchards.

name: Tauktae
description: The strongest cyclone to strike Gujarat in over two decades, occurring during the peak of the COVID-19 pandemic.

name: Yaas
description: Caused massive storm surges that breached embankments, leading to saline water flooding in agricultural lands.

name: Biparjoy
description: Had the longest lifespan of any cyclone in the Arabian Sea, eventually bringing rare heavy rains to the Rajasthan desert.
